 {"id":"bd-ty7.1.2","title":"Extract and normalize Agent Mail Rust narrative/content from README and docs","description":"Create a structured content map from /dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/README.md and key docs: problem framing, features, workflows, CLI/MCP surfaces, architecture, comparisons, and FAQ material.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:28.758440301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T22:53:56.052799566Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T22:53:56.052774008Z","close_reason":"Completed route-aligned narrative extraction with source anchors and proof-point inventory","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ty7.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:28.758440301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Scope
Use README + docs as narrative source material.

# Required output
- Section-level content map aligned to target routes.
- Candidate hero copy inputs and supporting proof points.
- Pull quotes and claims tagged with source sections.

# Acceptance
All major README sections are represented in a reusable content outline with no major blind spots.
","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:28Z"},{"id":132,"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Deliverable: Agent Mail Rust Narrative/Content Extraction

This content map converts `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust` README/docs into route-aligned website content inputs with source anchors.

## 1) Route-aligned content map

- `/` (home): hero value prop, problem/solution framing, capability highlights, proof strip, quick-install CTA.
  - Sources: `README.md` sections around product positioning + quick start + stress evidence.
- `/showcase`: TUI screens walkthrough, robot-mode workflows, deterministic demo narrative, overseer flow.
  - Sources: README TUI/robot sections + `docs/OPERATOR_RUNBOOK.md` demo flows.
- `/architecture`: topology, crate layering, storage model, invariants, Search V3 architecture.
  - Sources: README architecture sections + ADR/spec docs.
- `/getting-started`: install paths, first-run setup, agent config snippets, first workflow macros, preflight checklist.
  - Sources: README install/quickstart + runbook onboarding.
- `/glossary`: canonical terms for project/agent/thread/reservation/search modes/interface mode.
  - Sources: `docs/TUI_V2_CONTRACT.md`, interface/search specs.
- `/spec-explorer`: browsable spec taxonomy for interface mode, search quality gates, web parity, verify-live, rollout.
  - Sources: README spec index + `docs/SPEC-*.md` + ADRs.

## 2) Candidate hero copy inputs

- Positioning: "Mail-like coordination layer for AI coding agents".
- Problem: multi-agent edits collide without shared coordination primitives.
- Differentiators: Git-auditable archive + SQLite index + MCP tool/resource surface.
- Social proof hook: multi-agent/large-message real-world build walkthrough.
- CTA primitives: install, run `am`, start server, watch walkthrough, read docs.

## 3) Verifiable proof points for marketing modules

- Capability scale: 34 MCP tools + 20+ resources.
- Stress benchmark highlights (pass rates, throughput, batching behavior).
- Search V3 latency/index throughput metrics (from `benches/BUDGETS.md`).
- CLI baseline latency metrics (from `benches/BUDGETS.md`).
- Parity/conformance coverage evidence (from `FEATURE_PARITY.md`).

## 4) Pull-quote candidates

- "It’s like Gmail for your coding agents!"
- "Mail metaphor, not chat."
- "Git as the source of truth."
- "Advisory, not mandatory."
- "Resilient to agent death."
- "Total Auto / Just Works." (VISION)

## 5) Content consistency risks to resolve before publish

- TUI screen-count mismatch across docs (11 vs 12 vs 15 references).
- Interface-mode policy wording mismatch across docs/specs (opt-in environment controls vs older wording).

## 6) Source anchors used

- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/README.md`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/OPERATOR_RUNBOOK.md`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/TUI_V2_CONTRACT.md`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/SPEC-search-v3-query-contract.md`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/SPEC-interface-mode-switch.md`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/SPEC-search-v3-quality-gates.md`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/SPEC-web-ui-parity-contract.md`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/SPEC-verify-live-contract.md`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/ADR-002-single-binary-cli-opt-in.md`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/benches/BUDGETS.md`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/FEATURE_PARITY.md`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/VISION.md`
","created_at":"2026-03-04T22:53:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ty7.1.3","title":"Analyze Rust codebase for mechanism-level truths that visualizations must explain","description":"Derive implementation-grounded architecture from crates and modules (server, tools, db, storage, guard, search, cli/tui) to drive accurate visualization design.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:29.232511872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T22:53:56.137194531Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T22:53:56.137176867Z","close_reason":"Completed mechanism-level code truth map and visualization recommendations","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ty7.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:29.232511872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":12,"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Scope
Investigate concrete mechanism boundaries and flows:
- message write/read paths,
- reservation lifecycle,
- pre-commit guard behavior,
- search V3 hybrid pipeline,
- TUI/robot surfaces,
- worker/coalescer reliability behaviors.

# Acceptance
A mechanism map exists that ties each visualization idea to concrete source files and concepts.
","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:29Z"},{"id":133,"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Deliverable: Mechanism-Level Code Truth Map for Visualization Planning

This map ties visualization ideas to concrete code mechanisms in `mcp_agent_mail_rust`.

## 1) Messaging lifecycle (send + read)

- Send path: tool layer writes DB first, then indexes/signals, then best-effort archive enqueue.
  - Core modules: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/messaging.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs`.
- Read path: inbox fetch joins recipients/messages/sender, applies priority/since filters, then clears signal.
  - Core modules: `messaging.rs`, `queries.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/robot.rs`.
- Visualization recommendation: end-to-end sequence diagram with success/degraded branch and signal-clear timing.

## 2) Reservation lifecycle + conflict handling

- Acquire/release/renew/force-release are explicit tool surfaces with DB-backed overlap checks and stale-signal heuristics.
  - Core modules: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/reservations.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs`.
- Visualization recommendation: state machine (`active -> renewed/released/expired/forced`) + overlap/conflict graph.

## 3) Pre-commit guard architecture

- Install/uninstall tools orchestrate guard scripts against git hooks path/worktree context.
- Guard checks staged/push paths against active exclusive reservations (SQLite + archive checks, bypass/gate modes).
  - Core modules: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/reservations.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-guard/src/lib.rs`.
- Visualization recommendation: decision tree (`mode -> conflict? -> allow/warn/block`) with per-path rationale.

## 4) Search V3 pipeline

- Tool layer builds query envelope + explain facets.
- Service executes cached/lexical/semantic/hybrid branches with budget governor, rerank, scope finishing, degraded diagnostics.
- Planner still supports LIKE/cursor/facet fallback planning.
  - Core modules: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/search.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_planner.rs`.
- Visualization recommendation: pipeline Sankey (`query -> candidate sets -> merge/rerank -> scope/redaction -> response`) + degraded badges.

## 5) TUI runtime internals

- Runtime boot = shared state + DB poller + HTTP supervisor.
- Poller loop includes panic guarding, data-version short-circuiting, reservation-expiry refresh trigger, sqlite recovery gates.
- Bridge/event ring buffers + app-side deferred queues govern screen updates.
  - Core modules: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs`, `tui_poller.rs`, `tui_bridge.rs`, `tui_app.rs`.
- Visualization recommendation: live architecture graph (poller/supervisor/shared-state/screens + event backlog telemetry).

## 6) Robot-mode surfaces

- Envelope + output-format selection + command dispatch (status/inbox/search/reservations) with non-interactive default path.
  - Core modules: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/robot.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs`.
- Visualization recommendation: command track map and output-mode flow (`tty -> toon`, `pipe -> json`, markdown prose mode).

## 7) Reliability internals (WBQ + commit coalescer)

- WBQ: bounded enqueue, disk-pressure skipping, batched drain, transient retry, queue/latency metrics.
- Coalescer: worker pools, per-repo queues/spill buffers, fairness scheduling, lock-free-first commits + lock-retry cleanup path.
  - Core modules: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs`.
- Visualization recommendation: reliability dashboard with queue depth, lock contention, retry heatmaps, graceful-shutdown flush status.

## 8) High-value warning for website truthfulness

- `topic` parameter currently accepted by fetch path but not applied as an active filter in the demonstrated path. Treat as implementation nuance and avoid overclaiming topic-filter guarantees without end-to-end verification.

## 9) Primary source roots

- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/messaging.rs`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/reservations.rs`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/search.rs`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_planner.rs`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-guard/src/lib.rs`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/{lib.rs,tui_poller.rs,tui_bridge.rs,tui_app.rs}`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/{robot.rs,lib.rs}`
- `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs`
","created_at":"2026-03-04T22:53:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ty7.1.4","title":"Build claim-to-source traceability matrix for all high-visibility website assertions","description":"Create a matrix linking each planned major claim to exact repository evidence (file paths, docs sections, test evidence where applicable).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:29.621709601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T22:58:14.082073911Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T22:58:14.082053192Z","close_reason":"Completed high-visibility claim traceability matrix with confidence and wording constraints","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ty7.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:29.621709601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ty7.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:30.421036708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ty7.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:30.618382870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ty7.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:30.833141102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Why this is critical
The site will make strong technical claims. This matrix prevents accidental misrepresentation and simplifies future reviews/updates.

# Required fields
- claim text,
- evidence source path(s),
- confidence level,
- notes for wording constraints.

# Acceptance
Every major hero/feature/architecture claim has explicit evidence mapping.
","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:29Z"},{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Deliverable: Claim-to-Source Traceability Matrix

All high-visibility claims below map to concrete repository evidence and include wording constraints.

| Claim candidate (website-facing) | Evidence source(s) | Confidence | Wording constraints / caveats |
|---|---|---:|---|
| MCP Agent Mail provides a large tool/resource surface for agent workflows. | `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/README.md` (product overview, tools/resources counts), `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/FEATURE_PARITY.md` | High | Prefer “34 MCP tools + 20+ resources” and avoid hardcoding resource count if docs diverge; re-check before release. |
| Core metaphor is asynchronous mail-style coordination, not chat. | `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/README.md` (mail metaphor / principles section) | High | Use exact framing “mail metaphor, not chat”; do not describe as real-time chat system. |
| Git archive is the durable audit surface; SQLite is query/index acceleration. | `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/README.md` (source-of-truth principles), `crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs` | High | Avoid claiming SQLite is canonical source-of-truth. |
| File reservations are advisory coordination primitives with conflict detection. | `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/README.md` (principles), `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/reservations.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs` | High | Do not claim mandatory repo-wide enforcement; phrase as advisory + guard-integrated. |
| Pre-commit guard can enforce reservation checks during git workflows. | `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/reservations.rs` (install/uninstall guard tools), `crates/mcp-agent-mail-guard/src/lib.rs` | High | Phrase as “can enforce when installed/configured”, not always-on by default. |
| Message send path is transactional in DB, then index/signal, with best-effort archive enqueue. | `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/messaging.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs` | High | Avoid implying archive write is synchronous success requirement. |
| Inbox read path supports urgency/since windows and powers robot-mode triage. | `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/messaging.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/robot.rs` | High | Avoid claiming topic-filter guarantee without re-validating current implementation. |
| Search V3 supports lexical/semantic/hybrid branches with explain/degraded diagnostics. | `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/search.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_planner.rs` | High | Avoid “always semantic rerank”; present as branch/budget-governed pipeline. |
| TUI includes multi-screen operational workflows (mailboxes, reservations, statuses, etc.). | `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/README.md` (TUI section), `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/TUI_V2_CONTRACT.md`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_*.rs` | Medium | Screen-count references conflict across docs; avoid precise fixed count unless normalized first. |
| Robot mode provides non-interactive automation-oriented output for agent loops. | `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/robot.rs`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs`, README robot mode section | High | Avoid implying only JSON; mention format-aware output pathways. |
| Reliability internals include write-behind queue and commit coalescer mechanisms. | `crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs` | High | Present as internal reliability architecture; do not overstate hard real-time guarantees. |
| Stress/perf evidence exists and should be showcased as proof-of-utility. | `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/README.md` (stress gauntlet summary), `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/benches/BUDGETS.md` | High | Keep benchmark date/context with any numbers; avoid universal performance claims. |
| Quick-start onboarding can be “install -> init -> run server -> use tools/CLI/TUI.” | `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/README.md` install/quickstart sections, `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/OPERATOR_RUNBOOK.md` | High | Keep commands version-synchronized with README to avoid drift. |
| Interface mode supports CLI/TUI/web trajectories with explicit contracts/specs. | `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/SPEC-interface-mode-switch.md`, `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/docs/SPEC-web-ui-parity-contract.md`, ADR/spec docs | Medium | Docs have legacy wording conflicts; avoid strong claims of full parity until verified. |
| Product narrative can be backed by real multi-agent build/workflow evidence. | `/dp/mcp_agent_mail_rust/README.md` (walkthrough and case-study narrative), runbook sections | Medium-High | Keep evidence framed as exemplar case studies, not guaranteed outcomes for all teams. |

## Review constraints for downstream copy/viz beads

- Every hero stat, feature tile, and architecture callout must reference one of the sources above.
- Any claim not in this table requires either:
  1. New source evidence entry, or
  2. Wording downgrade to exploratory/non-assertive phrasing.
- Before launch, re-validate numeric claims against current README/bench files.
","created_at":"2026-03-04T22:58:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ty7.1.6","title":"Extract reusable storytelling/conversion patterns from frankentui_website","description":"Analyze /dp/frankentui_website for high-performing presentation patterns (centrepiece hero media, layered CTAs, proof/stat blocks, live-demo handoff, narrative pacing, and section composition) and map what should be adapted for Agent Mail marketing without copy drift.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:29:48.914541134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T22:53:56.222551168Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T22:53:56.222525039Z","close_reason":"Completed frankentui conversion-pattern extraction including hero video placeholder workflow","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ty7.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:29:48.914541134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"bd-ty7.1.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Deliverable: frankentui_website Conversion Pattern Extraction

This captures reusable storytelling/conversion patterns from `/dp/frankentui_website` for Agent Mail website adaptation.

## 1) Centerpiece media pattern (hero proof block)

- Pattern: headline + high-density media frame directly beneath, with metadata/proof overlays.
- Adaptation: landing hero should include dashboard video placeholder as centerpiece (replace later with user-provided recording), not decorative animation.

## 2) CTA layering strategy

- Pattern: repeated CTAs across journey (hero, mid-page, close, nav persistent CTA), not single conversion point.
- Adaptation: prioritized Agent Mail CTA ladder:
  1. Install command
  2. Start server / first run
  3. Watch walkthrough video
  4. View GitHub/docs

## 3) Social proof composition

- Pattern: combine quantitative stats + timeline/progress + testimonial/reaction modules.
- Adaptation: use capability metrics + stress-test evidence + quote cards from Agent Mail source docs.

## 4) Narrative pacing blueprint

- Pattern: hook -> proof demo -> differentiation -> evidence -> conversion.
- Adaptation for Agent Mail home:
  - Problem
  - Coordination model
  - Live/video proof
  - Stress results
  - Cross-agent workflow
  - Social proof
  - Install + quick start

## 5) Section composition contract

- Pattern: consistent section shell (sticky context rail + content rail), centralized data arrays, modular blocks.
- Adaptation: keep all narrative/control data in `lib/content.ts` and reuse same shell grammar.

## 6) Hero video placeholder + replacement workflow (explicit)

1. Define `heroMedia` in content model (`poster`, `mp4/webm`, proof-chip text, fallback link).
2. Use framed player block in hero component with immediate visual prominence.
3. Behavior defaults:
   - `poster` always present
   - `playsInline`
   - muted autoplay only for hero preview mode
4. Accessibility/perf fallback:
   - reduced-motion => poster + explicit "Watch walkthrough" CTA
   - load failure => static proof card fallback
5. Keep proof overlay visible even before playback so value proposition is retained when autoplay is blocked.

## 7) Primary reference roots

- `/dp/frankentui_website/app/page.tsx`
- `/dp/frankentui_website/components/section-shell.tsx`
- `/dp/frankentui_website/components/site-header.tsx`
- `/dp/frankentui_website/components/video-player.tsx`
- `/dp/frankentui_website/components/terminal-demo.tsx`
- `/dp/frankentui_website/components/{stats-grid.tsx,timeline.tsx,tweet-wall.tsx}`
- `/dp/frankentui_website/lib/content.ts`
","created_at":"2026-03-04T22:53:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ty7.2","title":"Scaffold parity with asupersync website (stack, shell, reusable primitives)","description":"Rebuild the technical scaffold in this repo so page composition and visualization work can proceed on a proven architecture.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:03.550942622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:01:38.477879271Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T23:01:38.477857700Z","close_reason":"Scaffold parity achieved: stack, shell, primitives, and centralized onboarding contracts implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ty7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ty7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:03.550942622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ty7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ty7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:06.662719435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-ty7.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Implementation strategy
Mirror asupersync architecture first, then apply minimal brand/theme deltas.

# Critical constraints
- Keep Next.js 16 + React 19 + TS strict + Tailwind 4 parity.
- Preserve dynamic-import strategy for heavy visualization components.
- Reuse shell components rather than inventing parallel patterns.
","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:03Z"},{"id":53,"issue_id":"bd-ty7.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Decomposition rationale\nScaffold work is intentionally separated from content/visualizations so foundational architecture decisions settle early. This reduces repeated page churn and keeps future refactors localized to shared primitives.\n\n# Key tradeoff\nHigh parity with reference architecture vs bespoke redesign. We deliberately prefer parity to minimize execution risk and preserve proven UX patterns.\n\n# Completion standard\nAll later beads should compose onto scaffold primitives rather than inventing alternate patterns.

 {"id":"bd-ty7.2.1","title":"Port stack/config parity from reference website","description":"Match the reference stack and baseline config: Next.js app-router setup, TypeScript strictness, Tailwind setup, lint scripts, and package management conventions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:44.175662461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T22:55:19.264621876Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T22:55:19.264597530Z","close_reason":"Ported asupersync stack/config scaffold and validated with bun install/lint/tsc","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ty7.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ty7.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:03:48.182509663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ty7.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ty7.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:44.175662461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":15,"issue_id":"bd-ty7.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Deliverables

","created_at":"2026-03-04T19:01:44Z"},{"id":136,"issue_id":"bd-ty7.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented stack/config scaffold parity by porting the asupersync website baseline into this repo.

## Work completed

- Ported foundational project structure:
  - `app/`, `components/`, `hooks/`, `lib/`, `public/`, `types/`
- Ported baseline config/tooling files:
  - `package.json`, `bun.lock`, `next.config.ts`, `tsconfig.json`, `eslint.config.mjs`, `postcss.config.mjs`, `next-env.d.ts`
- Installed dependencies with bun.
- Fixed one lint regression in ported baseline:
  - `components/custom-cursor.tsx` removed mount-only `setState` effect flagged by `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect`.

## Validation

- `bun install` ✅
- `bun lint` ✅
- `bun tsc --noEmit` ✅

## Notes for downstream beads

- This closes stack/config parity initialization and unblocks route/component adaptation work.
- Domain content and Agent Mail-specific visualizations still need to be replaced from asupersync defaults in subsequent beads.
